summary: Per-category email routing + extended calendar mapping verified end-to-end. Backend: 12/13 tests passed (1 skipped because public registration is disabled â€” that branch is covered by the no-token 401/403 test). Frontend: 5 calendar mapping dropdowns + 5-row Email Routing panel render correctly on /settings/integrations, all GMAIL_FROM_ALIASES surface as <option>s, and a saved value survives a full page reload.
what_was_tested: GET response shape: saved, resolved, categories=['assessments','scope','finance','projects','maintenance'], allowed_aliases contains all 5 role aliases + darren@.
PUT /api/settings/email-routing with 5 aliases persists and round-trips through subsequent GET.
PUT with a non-whitelisted From returns 400 with a clear 'whitelist' message.
email_routing.get_from_for_category returns saved value, falls back to env alias when blank (proved by clearing maintenance and re-reading).
email_sender.send_for_category, mocked at smtplib.SMTP, produces MIME with From header = scope@... for category 'scope' and finance@... for 'finance', plus a Message-ID on @sealtechsolutions.co.
GoogleCalendarSettings now exposes scope_calendar_id + finance_calendar_id; PUT /api/integrations/google/settings persists both and GET /api/integrations/google/status reflects them.
deal_events.push_event_to_gcal calendar picker: Roof Walkâ†’ASSESS, Presentationâ†’SCOPE, Meetingâ†’SCOPE, Job Startâ†’PROJECT, Otherâ†’ASSESS (verified by stubbing gcal.get_settings + upsert_event).
deal_events.send_due_reminders embeds the documented category map (Job Startâ†’projects, Presentation/Meetingâ†’scope, Roof Walkâ†’assessments, Otherâ†’projects) â€” verified by source inspection.

Code-level confirmation of category routing at all send sites: server.py (scope emailâ†’'scope' via get_from_for_category; POâ†’'projects'; invoiceâ†’'finance'; statementâ†’'finance'; payablesâ†’'finance'; library sendâ†’'projects' with explicit allowed-alias check; stale-deal digestâ†’send_for_category 'scope'); assessment.pyâ†’'assessments'; coi_reminders.pyâ†’'projects'; scheduler.py daily-statusâ†’'projects'.

Frontend: /settings/integrations renders 5 map-* dropdowns and the email-routing-panel with 5 select rows; selects list all aliases; saving a change persists across reload (verified by reading select.value after page.reload()).
critical_code_review_comments: [minor] React console warning on the Integrations page: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' â€” caused by emergent's runtime instrumentation injecting <span x-excluded> wrappers around the option text expression. Not a user-code bug, but worth knowing if hydration errors are ever reported in prod.

[nice-to-have] email_routing.EmailRoutingIn uses EmailStr but the whitelist check is case-insensitive â€” consistent. Consider returning the rejected category list in the 400 body as structured JSON (currently returned as detail string) for cleaner UI error display.

[nice-to-have] /settings/integrations does not currently visualize which category will fall back to env when a saved value is blank. Could surface the 'resolved' value as small helper text below each blank dropdown.

success_rate.backend: 100% (12/12 executed, 1 skipped due to disabled public registration)

seed_data_creation: Final state of app_settings.email_routing doc has all 5 categories set to their matching role aliases; the test_put_saves_and_persists test wrote them as the canonical mapping. Google calendar settings on the admin user were updated with scope_calendar_id='scope_test_cal@group.calendar.google.com' and finance_calendar_id='finance_test_cal@group.calendar.google.com' â€” these are dummy IDs; main agent should overwrite via the UI when real calendars are picked.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email routing is fully wired. If you touch send sites, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_email_routing.py â€” it inspects every call site in server.py / assessment.py / coi_reminders.py / scheduler.py / deal_events.py to make sure they keep routing by category. The push_event_to_gcal test stubs gcal.get_settings + upsert_event in place; if you refactor google_calendar.py, restore the original attrs after the test or duplicate the FakeSettings approach.
rca_of_the_issue: n/a â€” no bugs to RCA. All flows worked on first run.
